title: Intel Chairman Frank Yeary retires, Craig Barrat to become the new chairman of the Board of Directors
summary: Intel Chairman Frank Yeary has retired from the board of directors, replaced by Craig Barrat.
insight: Shift in leadership may impact Intel's future strategic decisions.

title: Seagate begins shipping 44TB hard drives with HAMR tech to data centers Mozaic 4+ platform expands to 10 platters
summary: Seagate has started shipping 44TB hard drives with HAMR tech to data centers, marking a major milestone in storage capacity. The Mozaic 4+ platform expansion to 10 platters also supports growing data needs. This move is expected to boost storage capacity in data centers.
insight: Data centers require exponentially increasing storage capacity.

title: AI memory crunch forces DRAM market into 'hourly pricing' model, report claims small and medium-sized businesses fighting for survival
summary: Small and medium-sized businesses struggle to absorb DRAM price increases, causing financial difficulties.
insight: Soaring memory costs threaten the survival of small businesses.

title: Intel's make-or-break 18A process node debuts for data center with 288-core Xeon 6+ CPU multi-chip monster sports 12 channels of DDR5-8000, Foveros Direct 3D packaging tech
summary: Intel introduces Xeon 6+ CPUs with up to 288 cores, made on 18A process technology. These powerful CPUs feature 12 channels of DDR5-8000 memory and utilize Foveros Direct 3D packaging technology for enhanced performance. This marks a significant upgrade for data centers.
insight: Massive core count boosts data center processing capabilities significantly.

title: Nvidia RTX 5070 mobile GPU looks set to get more VRAM despite global memory crisis 8GB could be boosted to 12GB, according to new listings
summary: Nvidia's RTX 5070 Mobile GPU may receive a memory boost from 8GB to 12GB of GDDR7 memory, according to manufacturer listings. This upgrade addresses concerns about the global memory crisis. The increased VRAM could improve gaming and VR performance.
insight: Potential 12GB upgrade addresses VRAM shortages in gaming laptops.

title: 2026 will bring sharpest PC declines in over a decade PC shipments to fall 10.4%
summary: PC shipments are expected to decline 10.4% in 2026, marking the sharpest decline in over a decade. This contraction is a significant concern for the tech industry. Analysts predict a steep decline in device shipments.
insight: Sharp PC decline indicates weakening consumer demand and market saturation.

title: Apple's new MacBook Air gets M5 and doubles starting storage base price increases to $1,099
summary: Apple's MacBook Air gets updated with M5 chip, increased starting price to $1,099, and doubled storage base.
insight: Apple raises MacBook Air starting price to boost profit margins further.

title: Drones attack several AWS Middle East region data centers amid Iran war, leading to outages service health been disrupted after power cut due to fire risk
summary: Amazon's Middle East data center business has been impacted by drone attacks during the 2026 Iran Conflict, resulting in outages and disruptions to service health. Power cuts were triggered due to a fire risk. The incident highlights the vulnerability of critical infrastructure in conflict zones.
insight: Critical infrastructure vulnerable to attacks in active conflict zones

title: Apple launches new MacBook Pros powered by M5 Pro, M5 Max, and 2x faster SSDs new 'Super Cores' help deliver up to 30 percent performance boost
summary: Apple's new MacBook Pros feature improved performance and storage, but higher prices.
insight: Significant performance boost at a premium price point.

title: Nvidia-backed trial shows AI data centers can flexibly adjust power use in near real time, with global implications for energy consumption suggests hyperscalers can reduce consumption as necessary, ensuring grid isn t overloaded during peak demand
summary: A U.K. study shows AI data centers can adjust power use in near real-time, reducing peak demands and potential grid overloads.
insight: Dynamic power management can prevent grid overloads during peak hours.

title: Biwin Black Opal X570 Pro SSD Review: An 8TB unobtanium monster
summary: The Biwin Black Opal X570 Pro is an 8TB SSD offering a compelling alternative to high-capacity drives. It provides large storage capacities and potentially lower costs, making it a viable option for users seeking extensive data storage. This drive is especially attractive for those seeking an alternative to Samsung's 8TB offerings.
insight: Large capacity SSDs become more affordable and accessible options.

title: South Korean authorities lose over $4.8 million in crypto after posting mnemonic recovery phrase online stolen PRTG tokens part of funds seized by National Tax Service from high-value tax evaders
summary: South Korean authorities lost over $4.8 million in crypto after posting a mnemonic seed phrase online, which was used to access a hardware wallet containing the stolen funds. The funds were seized by the National Tax Service from high-value tax evaders. The incident highlights a serious security lapse.
insight: Secrecy of mnemonic seed phrases must be prioritized in sensitive cases

title: New DNA HDD can be erased and overwritten repeatedly University of Missouri researchers aiming for next-gen thumb-drive-sized storage
summary: Researchers at the University of Missouri are developing a DNA HDD that can be erased and rewritten, bringing the technology closer to being a practical and rewritable storage system. This DNA-based storage has the potential to revolutionize data storage, offering high capacity and longevity. The team's work moves the field closer to a thumb-drive-sized storage device.
insight: Rewritable DNA storage could replace traditional data storage mediums

title: DRAM bots reportedly being deployed to hoover up memory chips and components one operation ran 10 million web scraping requests, hitting DDR5 RAM product pages every 6.5 seconds
summary: DataDome thwarted a large-scale scalping operation targeting memory modules, exacerbating the memory shortage.
insight: Massive scalping operations worsen memory shortages with artificially inflated prices.

title: Pocket $219 in savings when you buy this blistering 9800X3D, motherboard, and 32GB RAM bundle start your AM5 4K gaming rig for less than $1,000
summary: Get AMD's powerful 9800X3D processor, B850 motherboard, and 32GB RAM for under $1,000.
insight: Affordable high-performance gaming rig for 4K gaming enthusiasts.

title: Microsoft adds Shader Execution Reordering (SER) in latest DirectX SDK for more efficient ray tracing Intel Arc B-series GPUs show 90% performance uplift
summary: Microsoft's latest DXR 1.2 update includes Shader Execution Reordering (SER) and Opacity Micromaps (OMMs), enabling faster ray-traced scenes for game developers and GPU makers.
insight: Ray tracing performance to improve on Intel Arc B-series GPUs.

title: Upgraded FGH200M Wi-Fi HaLow module features Morse Micro MM8108 SoC, handles up to 8,191 IoT devices
summary: The Quectel FGH200M Wi-Fi HaLow module, based on Morse Micro MM8108 SoC, offers long-range, low-power IoT connectivity with a range of up to 1 km and the ability to connect up to 8,191 devices. It operates in industrial environments and supports various interfaces, including USB 2.0 and SPI. The module is suitable for applications such as smart homes and large-area IoT networks.
insight: Enhanced IoT connectivity for large-scale, industrial smart environments.

title: Dabao board features open-source hardware Baochip-1x RISC-V MCU (Crowdfunding)
summary: The Dabao board features an open-source Baochip-1x RISC-V MCU with a 350 MHz CPU core, 4MB ReRAM, 2MB SRAM, and various I/Os. The board is designed to be inspectable using the IRIS technique, allowing users to verify the silicon without damaging it. The Dabao board is available for crowdfunding.
insight: Breakthrough in open-source hardware with inspectable, secure, and customizable RISC-V MCU.

title: Renesas RA0E3 Arm Cortex-M23 MCU is a stripped-down RA0E1 for cost-sensitive applications
summary: Renesas RA0E3 is a stripped-down MCU for cost-sensitive applications with reduced memory and peripherals.
insight: Budget-friendly option for sensing, motor assist, and basic system control.

title: Qualcomm Snapdragon Wear Elite wearable platform offers 5G RedCap, WiFi 6, Bluetooth 6.0, built-in AI accelerator
summary: Qualcomm Snapdragon Wear Elite is a personal AI wearable platform featuring 5G, Wi-Fi 6, and a built-in AI accelerator, offering improved performance, battery life, and charging capabilities.
insight: Advances wearable technology with AI-powered performance and multi-day battery life.

title: pureLiFi Bridge XC Flex delivers gigabit broadband internet through windows (made of glass)
summary: pureLiFi Bridge XC Flex provides up to 1 Gbps broadband internet through windows without cabling, leveraging LiFi data transmission and wireless power transfer.
insight: Wireless broadband access through windows revolutionizes home internet connectivity.

title: ST s smart IMU bolsters Qualcomm s monster AI chip for wearables
summary: Qualcomm announced the Snapdragon Wear Elite chip, a powerful AI chip for wearables, set to appear in personal AI devices from Google, Motorola, and Samsung later this year.
insight: Qualcomm's AI chip boosts wearable technology with enhanced AI capabilities.

title: Leaked government-grade iPhone hacking tools now used to steal crypto and data from users
summary: Leaked government-grade iPhone hacking tools are being used to steal crypto and data from users, exploiting 23 iOS vulnerabilities.
insight: Government-grade hacking tools have been repurposed for massive cybercrime operations worldwide.

title: South Korea's tax office lost millions in crypto after accidentally posting the wallet's master key
summary: South Korea's tax office accidentally posted a crypto wallet master key online, losing millions. The National Tax Service released a statement about an investigation, including a photo that revealed sensitive information. This led to the loss of significant funds.
insight: Careless disclosure of sensitive information can have disastrous financial consequences.

title: Apple unveils MacBook Pro with M5 Pro and M5 Max, refreshes MacBook Air with M5
summary: Apple unveils MacBook Pro with M5 Pro and M5 Max, featuring Liquid Retina XDR displays and increased storage capacity. The new MacBook Pro will be available in 14-inch and 16-inch configurations. This refresh aims to improve performance and user experience.
insight: Enhanced performance and display quality in new MacBook Pro models.

title: A rewritable hard drive made of DNA? Researchers say it's possible
summary: Researchers are exploring DNA as a compact, stable digital storage medium. DNA strands can encode vast amounts of information. This could revolutionize data storage.
insight: Unlocking DNA's potential for ultra-dense, long-lasting digital storage.

title: Jolla Sailfish pitches a "European phone" for users wary of Google and Apple
summary: Jolla returns to smartphone market with a new device assembled in Finland, running on Sailfish OS, after a decade of turmoil and rebranding.
insight: Jolla's European flagship phone offers an alternative to Google and Apple.

title: OpenAI revises Pentagon contract to curb mass surveillance, but critics warn of major loopholes
summary: OpenAI revises Pentagon contract to address concerns over mass surveillance, citing a valuable lesson learned.
insight: OpenAI prioritizes transparency and accountability in high-stakes government partnerships.

title: Qualcomm is getting ready for Wi-Fi 8 this year, 6G in 2029
summary: Qualcomm is working with partners to launch 6G in 2029, starting with commercial deployments.
insight: Accelerating next-generation wireless technology for faster and more reliable connectivity.

title: ASML looks beyond EUV, plans new tools for larger chips and 3D packaging
summary: ASML is expanding its product line to include larger chip production tools and 3D packaging, moving beyond its current role as the world's sole EUV supplier.
insight: ASML's diversification fuels growth in AI-era chip manufacturing capabilities.

title: Those cheap Windows and Office keys might come from a federal crime
summary: A Florida woman has been sentenced to 22 months in prison for trafficking in unauthorized Microsoft products.
insight: Cheap Windows and Office keys may be linked to federal crimes.

title: Nvidia's plan to open-source 6G might be bad news for the companies that built 5G
summary: Nvidia announced a commitment to make 6G AI-native and open-source at Mobile World Congress. The initiative is backed by major telcos and companies like Ericsson and Nokia. This move could impact companies that built 5G infrastructure.
insight: Nvidia's open-source 6G plan threatens 5G industry investments

title: Starlink Mobile is rolling out V2 satellites that promise 100x data density from orbit
summary: Starlink is rolling out V2 satellites offering 100x data density from orbit, marking a significant upgrade to their connectivity service. This shift coincides with the rebranding of 'Direct to Cell' as Starlink Mobile, which enables smartphone connectivity without dedicated user terminals.
insight: Enhanced satellite technology enables widespread, high-speed mobile connectivity globally.

title: DirectX just made shader execution reordering official, boosting ray tracing performance in games
summary: DirectX has officially released Shader Model 6.9 with Shader Execution Reordering (SER), which boosts ray tracing performance in games. This feature had already been benefiting Nvidia and some game developers, but recent tests show significant improvements for Intel Arc Battlemage GPUs. Nearly doubling frame rates demonstrates SER's effectiveness.
insight: Shader Execution Reordering significantly boosts ray tracing performance in games.

title: Washington state bill would ban employers from forcibly microchipping workers
summary: Washington state's House Bill 2303 prohibits employers from microchipping employees. This bill aims to protect workers' rights and prevent potential privacy issues. It focuses on setting boundaries for employer-employee relationships.
insight: Protects workers from involuntary implantation of microchips in their bodies

title: Samsung Wallet can now store your house keys in your phone
summary: Samsung Wallet now supports digital home keys for select smart locks. Users can unlock their homes with their phone or smartwatch. This feature is rolling out starting this month.
insight: Seamless keyless entry enhances home security and convenience.

title: AI-driven warfare is here, and the Iran strikes show how fast it's advancing
summary: The US and Israel allegedly used Anthropic's Claude AI model in operations against Iran, applying it to intelligence analysis and scenario planning for targeting.
insight: AI is rapidly evolving in warfare, blurring lines of human control.

title: TCL brings AMOLED to its paper-like Nxtpaper concept phone
summary: TCL is upgrading its Nxtpaper concept phone from LCD to AMOLED panels, improving contrast and color depth.
insight: TCL enhances display quality for a better reading experience.

title: The Best Gaming Monitors... in $100 Increments
summary: The article reviews top gaming monitors in $100 increments, providing clear recommendations. It tests options from $150 to $1,500, helping users make informed purchasing decisions. The article offers no-frills, actionable advice for gamers.
insight: Gaming monitors with specific budgets can greatly enhance gaming experiences.
